steps.title: Spot the plural subject
steps.content: The subject is "Objects," which is plural. Although an appositive phrase interrupts after it, the subject's number doesn't change.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Rule: the verb must agree with "Objects" (plural), not with the singular nouns inside the interrupting appositive ("49036 Pelion, a 46-kilometer-wide body first detected in 1998").

A — "is exhibiting" Singular present progressive. Treats "Objects" as singular. Fails.

B — "exhibits" Singular simple present. Agrees with a singular subject, not the plural "Objects." Fails.

C — "has exhibited" Singular present perfect. Again treats "Objects" as singular. Fails.

D — "exhibit" Plural simple present. Agrees with the plural subject "Objects," correctly describing their shared characteristics. Correct.

D is correct.
